channel.title: SoftwareArchitecture on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in SoftwareArchitecture on emsenn.net

item.title: Architecture Decision Records
item.description:
Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) are short text documents that capture a single architectural decision, its context, and its consequences. Michael Nygard introduced the format in a November 2011 blog post titled “Documenting Architecture Decisions,” published on the Cognitect blog (Nygard, 2011). The post argued that agile projects need lightweight documentation that stays close to the code, rather than large design documents that fall out of date.

Format

Nygard’s original ADR format draws on the structure of Alexandrian patterns. Each record contains five sections:

item.title: Domain-Driven Design
item.description: Domain-Driven Design (DDD) is an approach to software development that organizes code and teams around a model of the business domain. Eric Evans introduced the approach in Domain-Driven Design: Tackling Complexity in the Heart of Software (Addison-Wesley, 2003). Evans argued that the primary complexity in most software projects is not technical but conceptual — it lies in the domain the software serves. DDD addresses this by making the domain model the central artifact around which development is organized.
